perturbation.original_text: (c) Constellation shall be solely responsible for all costs and expenses incurred in connection with the creation and/or production of all advertising signs and/or promotional materials covered by this Agreement unless otherwise specifically noted on Exhibit E, and such costs and expenses shall be borne by Constellation in addition to any amounts due under this Agreement. Constellation shall be invoiced by the HOF Entities for any such materials at the retail cost charged to the HOF Entities (or either of them) by the supplier, without mark-up. Payment with respect to such invoice will be due thirty (30) days following receipt by Constellation of such invoice. Constellation shall be solely responsible for the initial cost of installation of all advertising signs covered by this Agreement, and Constellation shall be responsible for the cost of installation of each advertising sign which is changed as a result of a change in the artwork desired by Constellation during the Term subsequent to its initial installation except in the case of temporary signs planned for eventual replacement with permanent signs. Constellation shall be invoiced by the HOF Entities for any installation costs incurred as a result of such advertising signage change at the retail cost charged to the HOF Entities (or either of them) by the installer, without additional markup. Payment with respect to such invoice will be due thirty (30) days following receipt. The HOF Entities shall be solely responsible for the maintenance of the advertising signs covered by this Agreement during the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) Constellation shall be solely responsible for all costs and expenses incurred in connection with the creation and/or production of all advertising signs and/or promotional materials covered by this Agreement unless otherwise specifically noted on Exhibit E, and such costs and expenses shall be borne by Constellation in addition to any amounts due under this Agreement. Constellation shall be invoiced by the HOF Entities for any such materials at the retail cost charged to the HOF Entities (or either of them) by the supplier, without mark-up. Payment with respect to such invoice will be due sixty (60) days following receipt by Constellation of such invoice. Constellation shall be solely responsible for the initial cost of installation of all advertising signs covered by this Agreement, and Constellation shall be responsible for the cost of installation of each advertising sign which is changed as a result of a change in the artwork desired by Constellation during the Term subsequent to its initial installation except in the case of temporary signs planned for eventual replacement with permanent signs. Constellation shall be invoiced by the HOF Entities for any installation costs incurred as a result of such advertising signage change at the retail cost charged to the HOF Entities (or either of them) by the installer, without additional markup. Payment with respect to such invoice will be due thirty (30) days following receipt. The HOF Entities shall be solely responsible for the maintenance of the advertising signs covered by this Agreement during the Term.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment deadline for promotional materials invoices from 30 days to 60 days, while maintaining the 30-day payment deadline for installation costs invoices within the same paragraph. This creates confusion and uncertainty regarding the correct payment deadline for invoices related to advertising signs and promotional materials, potentially leading to disputes over late fees and breach of contract claims.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Constellation shall be solely responsible for all costs and expenses incurred in connection with the creation and/or production of all advertising signs and/or promotional materials covered by this Agreement unless otherwise specifically noted on Exhibit E, and such costs and expenses shall be borne by Constellation in addition to any amounts due under this Agreement. Constellation shall be invoiced by the HOF Entities for any such materials at the retail cost charged to the HOF Entities (or either of them) by the supplier, without mark-up. Payment with respect to such invoice will be due sixty (60) days following receipt by Constellation of such invoice. Constellation shall be solely responsible for the initial cost of installation of all advertising signs covered by this Agreement, and Constellation shall be responsible for the cost of installation of each advertising sign which is changed as a result of a change in the artwork desired by Constellation during the Term subsequent to its initial installation except in the case of temporary signs planned for eventual replacement with permanent signs. Constellation shall be invoiced by the HOF Entities for any installation costs incurred as a result of such advertising signage change at the retail cost charged to the HOF Entities (or either of them) by the installer, without additional markup. Payment with respect to such invoice will be due thirty (30) days following receipt. The HOF Entities shall be solely responsible for the maintenance of the advertising signs covered by this Agreement during the Term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline for promotional materials invoices from 30 days to 60 days, while the last sentence in the same section maintains a 30-day payment deadline for installation costs invoices. This creates a direct in-text contradiction as both payment deadlines are specified within the same clause for different types of invoices, leading to uncertainty about the correct payment term. As the explanation notes, this is a strong inconsistency that could lead to disputes over late fees and breach of contract claims because a reasonable reader would be confused by the two deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: Annual Activation Funds are to be used in each calendar year. Unused funds are not rolled into future contract years.
perturbation.changed_text: Annual Activation Funds are to be used in each calendar year. Unused funds may be rolled into future contract years with written approval from HOF Entities.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that unused Annual Activation Funds can now be rolled into future contract years with written approval. This contradicts the previous statement that unused funds are not rolled over, creating ambiguity and potentially leading to disputes over how these funds should be handled. The paragraph now contains conflicting information regarding the use of unused Annual Activation Funds.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the advertising and other rights described herein, Constellation shall pay to the HOF Entities total combined sponsorship fees (the "Sponsorship Fees") and total combined annual activation fund proceeds (the "Annual Activation Fund Proceeds") in the amounts and on the dates set forth on Exhibit H, in addition to any other amounts required by the terms of this Agreement. Annual Activation Funds are to be used in each calendar year. Unused funds may be rolled into future contract years with written approval from HOF Entities. In the event Constellation fails to pay to the HOF Entities when due any sum required by this Agreement to be paid, whether pursuant to this Section 3.4 or otherwise, interest shall accrue from the date due on the unpaid amount at the rate of [***] per month or, if less, the highest rate permitted by law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, both stating rules about Annual Activation Funds. Originally, unused funds were explicitly not rolled over. The modification allows rollover with written approval, introducing a direct inconsistency with the original prohibition and within the same paragraph, therefore there is an in-text contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 6.5 Governing Law; Venue. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Ohio, without reference to principles of conflicts of law. Any suit or action filed or otherwise commenced in connection with this Agreement must be filed and litigated in an appropriate court located in the City of Canton, Ohio (provided, however, that if the suit or action involves a claim for which federal courts have exclusive jurisdiction, then such suit or action must be filed in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of the State of Ohio in Akron, Ohio), or such other venue as deemed appropriate by the HOF Entities. In the event of a dispute between an HOF Entity and Constellation regarding their rights and duties hereunder, the non-prevailing Party in any ensuing litigation shall pay the reasonable attorneys' fees and expenses of the prevailing Party (including costs of discovery and expert witness fees).
perturbation.changed_text: 6.5 Governing Law; Venue. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Ohio, without reference to principles of conflicts of law. Any suit or action filed or otherwise commenced in connection with this Agreement must be filed and litigated in an appropriate court located in the City of Canton, Ohio (provided, however, that if the suit or action involves a claim for which federal courts have exclusive jurisdiction, then such suit or action must be filed in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of the State of Ohio in Akron, Ohio), or such other venue as deemed appropriate by the Constellation. In the event of a dispute between an HOF Entity and Constellation regarding their rights and duties hereunder, the non-prevailing Party in any ensuing litigation shall pay the reasonable attorneys' fees and expenses of the prevailing Party (including costs of discovery and expert witness fees).
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction within the Venue clause by first stating the venue is determined by the HOF Entities and then changing it to be determined by Constellation. This directly contradicts the previous clause, leading to uncertainty about which party has the right to choose the venue for legal disputes and creating a source of conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.5 Governing Law; Venue. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Ohio, without reference to principles of conflicts of law. Any suit or action filed or otherwise commenced in connection with this Agreement must be filed and litigated in an appropriate court located in the City of Canton, Ohio (provided, however, that if the suit or action involves a claim for which federal courts have exclusive jurisdiction, then such suit or action must be filed in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of the State of Ohio in Akron, Ohio), or such other venue as deemed appropriate by the Constellation. In the event of a dispute between an HOF Entity and Constellation regarding their rights and duties hereunder, the non-prevailing Party in any ensuing litigation shall pay the reasonable attorneys' fees and expenses of the prevailing Party (including costs of discovery and expert witness fees).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original venue clause, assigning venue selection to 'Constellation' instead of 'HOF Entities'. This creates a direct conflict within the Venue clause itself, as it initially stated HOF Entities had the right to determine venue, which is then directly contradicted by stating Constellation has that right. The contradicted text is the same text that was modified, exhibiting a strong in-text contradiction as described by the explanation.
